aboutMe.title: Karibuni! Welcome (plural)!
aboutMe.intro: I am a passionate Swahili teacher and tutor with over 17 years of experience helping learners of all ages develop confidence and fluency in Kiswahili.
body: Whether you are taking your first steps as a beginner or refining your advanced language skills, I provide engaging, practical, and personalized lessons designed around your unique learning goals.

I hold a Bachelor's Degree from Kenyatta University, one of Kenya's leading institutions. To continually enhance my teaching practice, I completed a Refresher Course in Kiswahili Teaching at the University of Dar es Salaam and participated in the African Languages Teaching Workshop organized by the NALRC Summer Institute in 2024.

My teaching style is fun, interactive, and personalized. I believe language is best learned through meaningful communication, practical activities, and appreciation of the rich cultures where Kiswahili is spoken. Every lesson is tailored to your pace, interests, and objectives.

I teach children, teenagers, and adults, offering lessons both online and in person. Whether you are learning for travel, business, school, work, or personal enrichment, I will help you build confidence to use Swahili in real-life situations.
My goal is simple: to make learning Swahili enjoyable, accessible, and rewarding while helping every learner achieve lasting confidence in the language.
Karibuni tujifunze Kiswahili pamoja! Welcome all - let's learn Swahili together!

Book a free trial lesson. I would love to help you begin or continue your Swahili learning journey. Together we will discuss your goals, assess your current level, and create a personalized learning plan that works for you.
I look forward to meeting you and helping you speak Swahili with confidence!
points: Cultural insights and practical communication skills

aboutSwahili.title: Swahili (Kiswahili): A Language That Connects Africa
aboutSwahili.intro: Swahili, also known as Kiswahili, is a Bantu language that originated along the East African coast and has grown into one of the most widely spoken languages in Africa.
body: Today, an estimated 100 to 150 million people speak Swahili worldwide. It is used across much of East and Central Africa, including Kenya, Tanzania, Uganda, Rwanda, Burundi, the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), Malawi, Zambia, Mozambique, Somalia, the Comoros, and South Africa.

Swahili holds official status in several countries: Kenya (official language alongside English), Tanzania (both national and official language), Uganda (official language alongside English), and the DRC (one of four national languages alongside Kikongo, Lingala, and Tshiluba).
Swahili also serves as the lingua franca of the East African Community (EAC), enabling people from different linguistic backgrounds to communicate effectively.

The language has gained continental and international recognition. In 2004, Swahili became one of the official languages of the African Union (AU), making it the first African language to receive this status. In 2020, South Africa approved the teaching of Swahili in its schools, reflecting its growing importance across the continent.
Today, Swahili continues to expand in influence as a language of education, business, diplomacy, tourism, and culture, connecting millions of people across Africa and beyond.
points: Official language in Kenya, Tanzania, and Uganda
National language in the DRC and lingua franca in the EAC
Taught in South African schools since 2020

programs.title: Choose Your Path / Chagua Njia Yako
programs.intro: From private coaching to team training, each program is built around goals you can actually use in the real world.

items.title: Private Coaching / Mafunzo Binafsi
items.description: 1:1 sessions tailored for relocation, travel, or confidence building in daily conversations.

items.title: Family Program / Programu ya Familia
items.description: Playful sessions for parents and children learning together through stories, games, and songs.

items.title: Corporate Track / Mafunzo ya Kampuni
items.description: Professional vocabulary, workplace etiquette, and roleplay scenarios for teams in Nairobi.

items.title: Community Immersion / Mazoezi ya Jamii
items.description: Practice weeks with guided meetups, market missions, and cultural storytelling circles.

dunia.sectionLabel: Dunia Wiki Hii • This Week in the World
dunia.title: Global Stories, Local Language / Habari za Dunia kwa Kiswahili
dunia.intro: Use current topics to build modern vocabulary you can use in real conversations this week.

items.title: AI in Schools / Akili Unde Mashuleni
items.description: Learn terms for policy, innovation, and education reform as AI tools expand in classrooms.

items.title: Urban Rain Plans / Mipango ya Mvua Mijini
items.description: Practice weather and infrastructure vocabulary through East African flood-prevention news.

items.title: Festival Season / Msimu wa Tamasha
items.description: Talk about music, fashion, and identity with bilingual prompts from regional festival stories.

calendar.title: Your Week in Swahili / Wiki Yako kwa Kiswahili
calendar.intro: Plan your lessons and meetups using day and month names in Kiswahili.

testimonials.quote: I moved to Nairobi knowing zero Swahili. Six months later I'm chatting with my neighbors and bargaining at Gikomba. This community changed my life.
testimonials.name: Sophie Laurent
testimonials.subtitle: French expat, 8 months in Nairobi

testimonials.quote: The meetups are the highlight of my month. You practice in a relaxed setting and leave with new friends. The teachers are patient and genuinely passionate.
testimonials.name: David Kimura
testimonials.subtitle: Japanese-Kenyan, Intermediate level

testimonials.quote: The business Swahili course helped me close deals faster and connect authentically with Kenyan clients. Worth every shilling and more.
testimonials.name: Aisha Mohamed
testimonials.subtitle: Business consultant, Nairobi CBD
